title: 5 Best Substack Alternatives in 2025 (Free & Paid)
meta_description: Looking to replace Substack? Compare the best email marketing platforms for newsletters, creators, and publishers with pricing and features.
intro: While Substack offers a straightforward platform for monetizing newsletters, alternatives may better suit your needs depending on your audience size, automation requirements, or monetization strategy. Whether you're seeking more advanced features, better customer support, or lower revenue-share fees, these alternatives provide compelling options for writers, creators, and publishers.
why_switch: Access more advanced automation and segmentation tools not available in Substack's core offering
Take advantage of lower fees or different pricing models that better suit your revenue goals
Leverage built-in ad networks and referral programs for additional monetization beyond subscriptions

alternatives.name: Beehiiv
alternatives.tagline: The newsletter platform built for growth
alternatives.key_difference: Beehiiv directly competes with Substack but offers an integrated ad network and referral program for additional revenue streams beyond paid subscriptions. With higher free tier limits (2,500 subscribers) and superior customer ratings, it's ideal for publishers seeking growth-focused tools.

alternatives.name: ConvertKit
alternatives.key_difference: ConvertKit specializes in visual automation and subscriber tagging, making it superior for creators who want to segment audiences and build automation sequences. It offers commerce functionality for selling digital products directly to your audience.

alternatives.name: MailerLite
alternatives.tagline: Simple email marketing for small businesses
alternatives.best_for: Small businesses and freelancers on a budget
alternatives.key_difference: MailerLite combines affordability (starting at $9/month) with powerful features like landing pages, pop-ups, and surveys all included in paid plans. It's perfect for creators wanting to grow their audience and convert subscribers without paying high subscription fees.

alternatives.name: ActiveCampaign
alternatives.best_for: Businesses needing advanced automation and CRM
alternatives.key_difference: ActiveCampaign goes beyond email to include integrated CRM, lead scoring, and site tracking capabilities. It's best for publishers who want to manage customer relationships and sales pipelines alongside their newsletter operations.

alternatives.name: GetResponse
alternatives.best_for: Online course creators and webinar hosts
alternatives.key_difference: GetResponse uniquely combines email marketing with built-in webinar hosting and online course functionality, making it ideal for creators who want to sell courses or host webinars alongside their newsletter. The integrated platform eliminates the need for multiple subscriptions.

faq.q: What is the best free alternative to Substack?
faq.a: Beehiiv offers the most generous free tier with up to 2,500 subscribers and built-in monetization features through its ad network. ConvertKit and MailerLite also have free plans supporting 1,000 subscribers each, though with slightly more limited features than Beehiiv.

faq.q: What is the best Substack alternative for small business?
faq.a: MailerLite is excellent for small businesses due to its affordable pricing starting at just $9/month combined with comprehensive features like landing pages, pop-ups, and e-commerce integrations. For businesses needing customer relationship management alongside email, ActiveCampaign provides integrated CRM functionality at a competitive price.

faq.q: Is there a cheaper alternative to Substack?
faq.a: Yes—MailerLite starts at $9/month compared to Substack's 10% revenue share on paid subscriptions, making it financially better for many publishers. GetResponse ($19/month) and Brevo ($25/month) also offer fixed pricing that may be cheaper depending on your subscriber count and revenue.
